always.name: Valid ID / Passport
always.description: Ensure your passport is valid for at least 6 months beyond your travel dates. Many countries require this.

always.name: Travel Insurance Policy
always.description: Highly recommended for all trips. Covers medical emergencies, trip cancellation, lost luggage, and more.

always.name: Booking Confirmations
always.description: Print or save digital copies of hotel, flight, train, and activity bookings.

always.name: Emergency Contact List
always.description: Written list of emergency contacts, embassy numbers, and local emergency services (police, ambulance).

international.name: Valid Passport
international.description: Required for all international travel. Check validity -- many countries require 6+ months remaining.

international.name: Visa (if required)
international.description: Check visa requirements for your destination. Some countries offer visa-on-arrival or e-visas. Apply well in advance.

international.name: Vaccination Certificate
international.description: Some countries require proof of vaccinations (Yellow Fever, COVID-19, etc.). Check WHO and destination requirements.

international.name: International Driving Permit
international.description: Required if you plan to rent a car abroad. Obtain one from your local automobile association before departure.

international.name: Travel Insurance (International)
international.description: Essential for international travel. Ensure it covers medical evacuation, especially for remote destinations.

international.name: Foreign Currency / Travel Cards
international.description: Bring some local currency for arrival. Consider a travel card with low foreign transaction fees.

international.name: Customs Declaration Forms
international.description: Some countries require you to declare items at customs. Be aware of duty-free allowances.

international.name: Embassy Registration
international.description: Register with your embassy or consulate at your destination. Useful in case of emergency or natural disaster.

domestic.name: Valid ID (National ID / Driver's License)
domestic.description: Carry government-issued photo ID for check-ins, car rentals, and age-restricted venues.

domestic.name: Health Insurance Card
domestic.description: Bring your health insurance card or details. Check if your plan covers you in other regions/states.

domestic.name: Driver's License
domestic.description: Required if you plan to drive or rent a car.

plane: Airline loyalty card / Frequent flyer number
TSA PreCheck / Global Entry card (if applicable)
